steps.title: Find the pronoun antecedent
steps.content: The pronoun in the blank refers to Marcel Duchamp's abstract cubist aesthetic. The antecedent "aesthetic" is singular and third person, so the pronoun must also be singular and third person.

steps.title: Reject plural and speaker pronouns
steps.content: A is plural and does not agree with "aesthetic." B is also plural. C introduces a first-person speaker not present in the sentence. A singular third-person pronoun correctly fits "represented a radical departure." The answer is D.
